guidance.text:
Primary - means no heart problem has yet occurred, such as an MI or Stroke.

Secondary - means the patient is known to have had CHD, PAD, Ischaemic Stroke, CAD or an MI in the past.

guidance.location: yellow sticky note, upper left near "Under Specialist Clinic?"
yellow sticky note, upper middle left near specialist referral branch
yellow note, upper middle right beside "Consider Lipid Clinic Referral"

guidance.text:
We assume 'Normal' to be 5 or below
(can vary from clinic to clinic)
guidance.location: yellow sticky note, center left near "Normal or Raised?"
yellow note, upper middle right beside top "Send "Lipid Lowering Therapy" AccuRx with self booking link" box
guidance.linked_to: Send "Lipid Lowering Therapy" AccuRx with self booking link

guidance.location: yellow sticky note, lower left near "Mark as "No Further Action""

guidance.text:
Maximally Tolerated Treatment - Treatment Order

Primary Prevention:
• Statin low dose e.g. Atorvastatin 20mg (if intolerant, should have tried 2 statins)
• Ezetimibe should be added on to statin/replace if intolerant to statin
• Ezetimibe/Bempedoic Acid should be escalated to from Ezetimibe if needed

Secondary Prevention:
• Statin high dose e.g. Atorvastatin 80mg (if intolerant, should have tried 2 statins)
• 2nd line if LDL ≥2.6 is Inclisiran
  - If this has happened and LDL >2 - Ezetimibe as Third Line.
• 2nd line if LDL 2-2.5, is Ezetimibe
guidance.subworkflow_brief: This panel defines a medication escalation subworkflow for lipid-lowering treatment. Inputs would include prevention category (primary vs secondary), current/statin tolerance status, LDL result, and current therapy stage. Logic: first classify the patient as primary or secondary prevention. For primary prevention, start with low-dose statin such as atorvastatin 20 mg; if statin intolerant, confirm that 2 statins have been tried, then use ezetimibe instead of or in addition to statin; if further lowering is needed, escalate from ezetimibe to ezetimibe/bempedoic acid. For secondary prevention, start with high-dose statin such as atorvastatin 80 mg; if intolerant, confirm 2 statins have been tried. Then use LDL thresholds to determine next steps: if LDL is 2–2.5, use ezetimibe as second line; if LDL is ≥2.6, use inclisiran as second line; if inclisiran has already been used and LDL remains >2, add ezetimibe as third line. Output would be the recommended next treatment step and whether the patient is already on maximally tolerated therapy.

guidance.text:
Inclisiran before Ezetimibe in Secondary Prevention?

Ezetimibe can be used as long as the LDL >2.0.

Inclisiran can only be used for secondary prevention and only if the LDL ≥ 2.6.

Using Ezetimibe first can bring the LDL to lower than 2.6, making Inclisiran unusable, however in this scenario while the LDL be remaining at >2, the patient would not be adequately treated to QOF target.
guidance.subworkflow_brief: This panel describes a treatment-order decision subworkflow specifically for secondary prevention patients choosing between inclisiran and ezetimibe. Inputs would include prevention category, current LDL value, and whether the objective is to preserve eligibility for inclisiran while still reaching target. Logic: confirm the patient is in secondary prevention; check whether LDL is ≥2.6, which is the threshold for inclisiran eligibility; note that ezetimibe is allowable whenever LDL is >2.0, but if used first it may lower LDL below 2.6 and thereby remove eligibility for inclisiran; if LDL remains >2 after that, the patient could still be above QOF target despite no longer qualifying for inclisiran. Output would be a sequencing recommendation: prefer inclisiran before ezetimibe when LDL is ≥2.6 in secondary prevention if preserving access to inclisiran is clinically important.

guidance.text:
Is the patient's treatment optimised?
Successful treatment is achieving the below:
• Reduction of >40% in the Non-HDL Cholesterol (Primary Prevention Target)
• Ideally Total Cholesterol <4.0 (Primary and Secondary Prevention Target)
• LDL ≤2 (Secondary Prevention Target)
• Non-HDL Cholesterol of <2.6 (Secondary Prevention Target)
guidance.subworkflow_brief: This panel defines an assessment subworkflow for determining whether lipid treatment is optimised. Inputs would include prevention category, baseline and current non-HDL cholesterol, current total cholesterol, current LDL, and current non-HDL cholesterol. Logic: compute the percentage reduction in non-HDL cholesterol from baseline; for primary prevention, check whether reduction is greater than 40%, while also considering ideal total cholesterol <4.0; for secondary prevention, evaluate total cholesterol <4.0, LDL ≤2, and non-HDL cholesterol <2.6. The workflow would likely classify optimisation status based on whether the relevant targets for that prevention group are met. Output would be a boolean or status label such as optimised/not optimised, plus possibly a list of unmet targets driving treatment escalation.

items.text: DFS plan: Root process 'Assess Total Cholesterol Result' with edges >7.5 -> decision 'Under Specialist Clinic?' (Yes -> end 'No Further Action'; No -> decision 'Are They Optimised on treatment?' [specialist path]), and <=7.5 -> decision 'Normal or Raised?' (Normal -> end 'Mark as Satisfactory'; Raised -> decision 'Taking Lipid Lowering Therapy?').

DFS plan: Specialist path decision 'Are They Optimised on treatment?' has Yes -> process 'Consider Lipid Clinic Referral' -> end 'Refer to specialist'; No -> process 'Send Lipid Lowering Therapy AccuRx with self booking link' -> end 'Consultation'.

DFS plan: Raised path decision 'Taking Lipid Lowering Therapy?' has No -> decision 'Primary or Secondary Prevention?' with Primary -> calculation/process 'Calculate QRISK2/3' -> decision '>=10%' (true -> process 'Send Lipid Lowering Therapy AccuRx with self booking link' -> merge to process 'Add in Ezetimibe'; false -> process 'Send High Cholesterol - Low QRISK AccuRx with self booking link' end), and Secondary -> decision 'Taking Maximally Tolerated Statin?'

DFS plan: Raised path decision 'Taking Lipid Lowering Therapy?' has Yes -> decision 'Are They Optimised on treatment?' [therapy path] with Yes -> end 'Mark as No Further Action'; No -> process 'Send Lipid Lowering Therapy AccuRx with self booking link' end.

DFS plan: Secondary prevention branch decision 'Taking Maximally Tolerated Statin?' has No -> process 'Send Lipid Lowering Therapy AccuRx with self booking link' end; Yes -> process 'Assess LDL' with LDL <=2 -> end 'No Further Action', LDL 2-2.5 -> process 'Add in Ezetimibe', LDL >=2.6 -> process 'Initiate Inclisiran' -> decision 'LDL >2.0' after repeat lipids at 3 months (false/No -> stop unresolved in image, true/Yes -> decision 'Has had 6 months of Inclisiran' leading to process 'Add in Ezetimibe').

Register variables from image before building: Total Cholesterol, Under Specialist Clinic, Optimised on treatment, Taking Lipid Lowering Therapy, Prevention Category, QRISK percent, Taking Maximally Tolerated Statin, LDL, Has had 6 months of Inclisiran.

DFS plan: Root process 'Assess Total Cholesterol Result' with edges >7.5 -> decision 'Under Specialist Clinic?' (Yes -> end 'No Further Action'; No -> decision 'Are They Optimised on treatment?' [specialist path]), and <=7.5 -> decision 'Normal or Raised?' (Normal -> end 'Mark as Satisfactory'; Raised -> decision 'Taking Lipid Lowering Therapy?').

DFS plan: Specialist path decision 'Are They Optimised on treatment?' has Yes -> process 'Consider Lipid Clinic Referral' -> end 'Refer to specialist'; No -> process 'Send Lipid Lowering Therapy AccuRx with self booking link' -> end 'Consultation'.

DFS plan: Raised path decision 'Taking Lipid Lowering Therapy?' has No -> decision 'Primary or Secondary Prevention?' with Primary -> calculation/process 'Calculate QRISK2/3' -> decision '>=10%' (true -> process 'Send Lipid Lowering Therapy AccuRx with self booking link' -> merge to process 'Add in Ezetimibe'; false -> process 'Send High Cholesterol - Low QRISK AccuRx with self booking link' end), and Secondary -> decision 'Taking Maximally Tolerated Statin?'

DFS plan: Raised path decision 'Taking Lipid Lowering Therapy?' has Yes -> decision 'Are They Optimised on treatment?' [therapy path] with Yes -> end 'Mark as No Further Action'; No -> process 'Send Lipid Lowering Therapy AccuRx with self booking link' end.

DFS plan: Secondary prevention branch decision 'Taking Maximally Tolerated Statin?' has No -> process 'Send Lipid Lowering Therapy AccuRx with self booking link' end; Yes -> process 'Assess LDL' with LDL <=2 -> end 'No Further Action', LDL 2-2.5 -> process 'Add in Ezetimibe', LDL >=2.6 -> process 'Initiate Inclisiran' -> decision 'LDL >2.0' after repeat lipids at 3 months (false/No -> stop unresolved in image, true/Yes -> decision 'Has had 6 months of Inclisiran' leading to process 'Add in Ezetimibe').

Register variables from image before building: Total Cholesterol, Under Specialist Clinic, Optimised on treatment, Taking Lipid Lowering Therapy, Prevention Category, QRISK percent, Taking Maximally Tolerated Statin, LDL, Has had 6 months of Inclisiran.
